quote_of_day.text:
>>6089
While I miss BhaCh days, I think it acted as a good purge, all who remain are true hardline imagebhangis all the redditards and musalbaiters have died out
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of caste hatred persisted over the board, with a front of feminist critiques blowing in from the northwest. Anon claimed feminism is 'based and aryanpilled,' sparking a squall of replies.

A fog of conspiracy theories rolled in during the afternoon, with Anon questioning Indian media control and alleging Jewish influence. The discussion was dense and unclear, with no visibility for hours.

A scattered drizzle of identity politics complaints continued throughout the day, with Anon lamenting their Bollywood and video game upbringing in India.

A cold snap of ethnic and nationalist tensions also moved in, with various claims and counter-claims about Biharis, Bengalis, and South Indians being Dalit. A heat wave of ego and boastfulness flared up briefly, as Anon claimed to have shaped Indiachan.

articles.title: Feminism and Caste Claims on Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon posted that 'Feminism is based and aryanpilled. If you hate feminism then you are a dalit. No true Uppercaste chad hate women or feminism.' This statement sparked a series of responses.

Anon countered that feminism literally means equal rights and any righteous person would agree.

Another anon disagreed, stating that feminism is a 'Judeo-American conspiracy to increase their profits and keep men as Goyims.' One respondent claimed Aryans never discriminated against women and that feminism is 'aryanpilled,' suggesting that people mistake dalit bias against Aryan men as feminism.

Another perspective emerged, with an anon arguing that modern 'feminism' is just 'kikery' and not what feminism originally was. Some posters connected this to the idea that elites want to divide and psyop men and women.

Several anons made claims about the relationship between caste and support for feminism. One stated that all real upper-caste men support feminism, while dalits, described as 'subhumans,' do not.

In contrast, another poster asserted Aryans are pro-feminism, while Dravidians hate it. The discussion included several anti-Semitic and misogynistic remarks. Anons also made assertions about the criminal behavior of dalits and the supposed suffering of Aryan men.

Some respondents criticized these views, while others engaged with the claims. The conversation reflects a complex interplay of casteist, anti-Semitic, and misogynistic views, with posters advancing conflicting claims about feminism and social dynamics in India.

articles.title: Anon questions Indian media control, alleges Jewish influence
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread questioning whether Indian media is controlled by Jews.

The original poster claimed that a translation of a statement about a "black man" robbing someone's brother was altered to "group of men," suggesting that Indian media "sucks up" to "niggers" despite their minimal presence in India.

One respondent agreed, stating that Indian media is "sucking up some American nigger 1000km Away and censoring the actual truth." Another claimed that the translation software used might be of Jewish make.

A third anon opined that "every country on this godforsaken planet is controlled by kikes in one way or another." Some anons discussed the potential influence of Jewish people on Indian media.

One claimed that Indian media is "owned by jews," citing the coverage of Ukraine as evidence. Another suggested that the influence of Jewish people is part of a broader "globohomo" or global homogenization of liberal values.

The thread also included several posts with brief, seemingly unrelated statements or links.

One anon shared a YouTube video, while another mentioned that "Not everything suggests centralized control" and that influence could come from shared liberal values rather than centralized control.

The discussion also touched on the idea of a "Black New World Order," with one anon stating that it "is unstoppable and inevitable." Several anons used derogatory language when referring to Jewish people, using terms like "kikes" and "jeets."

articles.title: Anon Laments Bollywood and Video Game Upbringing in India
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration about being born in India, where they claim they were forced to watch Bollywood films like Mughal-e-Azam and be told they were masterpieces.

They contrasted this with growing up in Western countries, where children play popular video games like Halo, GTA, and Call of Duty. Another anon responded, calling the mentioned video games 'slop' and sharing their fondness for the game WAW.

This respondent also criticized Bollywood war movies for being tactically and operationally inaccurate, as well as historically flawed. One anon described Call of Duty as 'kike propaganda' fed to American kids to prepare them to join the US army.

A different respondent mentioned that the Bollywood movie Shaurya is one of the few realistic army movies produced in India, despite being anti-army.

The original poster also received personal jabs from other anons, with one suggesting they deal with their circumstances and another questioning their qualifications and caste. The conversation also involved personal attacks and insults between respondents.

Anons exchanged views on video games, Bollywood, and Indian culture, with some defending their preferences and others criticizing them. One respondent mentioned that India does produce movies like Lakshya, which was an ad for army recruitment.

The discussion remained critical of various aspects of Indian culture and media, with anons sharing their dissatisfaction and frustration with their upbringing and surroundings.

articles.title: Anon claims Biharis, Bengalis, and South Indians are Dalit
articles.body:
Anon posted that Biharis, Bengalis, and South Indians are Dalit regardless of caste or surname. Another anon replied, calling themselves a 6'1" Tamil who claimed to "mog" all caste-kangers and said they were whiter than most pajeets.

A respondent called a Hyderabad Muslim a "pasmanda muthlim" and told them to "apni hala chudwa le katwe".

Anon also made derogatory comments about Biharis and South Indians, with one saying Biharis are the "biggest subhuman after southies".

articles.title: Anon Claims to Have Shaped Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon posted a list of slurs targeting various groups.

One respondent felt the current board composition was a result of a "good purge" that weeded out "redditards and musalbaiters." Another Anon agreed that the current and previous boards were similar but noted the older one had "good baits." Anon also claimed to have essentially created the current board, stating "So 90% of this chan is basically what I made it." Others responded with brief comments and reactions.
